cycle_disposition.description: Interpreter's judgment on the cycle. 'material_change' = at least one module has new content; 'null_cycle' = evidence bundle carries no material change; 'partial_cycle' = mixed; 'error_cycle' = reserved for engine-level failure paths (engine stamps, prompt does not emit).

cycle_disposition_reason.description: One-sentence justification. Required for null_cycle and partial_cycle; optional otherwise.

replay_mode.description: Sprint 3 Step 4a.3 (AD-2026-04-23 Option C). Engine-stamped true when WEEKLY_FILE_OVERRIDE was active on the dispatch. Absent/false in production dispatches. Not model-derived; the Interpreter LLM does not emit this field.

replay_window.description: Sprint 3 Step 4a.3 (AD-2026-04-23 Option C). Required iff replay_mode is true. Holds the replay cycle window read from the override weekly bundle's `_meta.week_ending` (end) and end-6d (start). Not model-derived.

end.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Copied from the override bundle's _meta.week_ending.

null_signal.description: BRIEF BX-3: explicit module-level absence flag. True when this module body is empty for this cycle. Optional; omitted when the module carries content.

empty_reason.description: BRIEF BX-3: short reason explaining why this module body is empty (e.g. 'interpreter_no_signal'). Required when null_signal is true.

absence_basis.description: BRIEF BX-3: which stage stamped this absence (e.g. 'interpreter_module_level', 'module_null_signal'). Free-form pending BRIEF 4 enum.

structured_claims.description: Triple store of every factual assertion in this interpretation. Populated by the Interpreter; consumed by the Composer (for claim_trace[] provenance) and the Reviewer gate (Step 2).

claim_id.description: Stable within a cycle. Format: aim-int-{ISO-date}-{4-digit-sequence}.

evidence_id.description: References weekly-research source_id or KB entry_id.

source_module.description: Which module or field the claim first appears in.

gaps_register.description: Optional. What evidence would improve Uncertain claims.

proposed_patches.description: Sprint 3 Step 4a.2 (AD-2026-04-23l §4.2) — Appendix A patch emission. OPTIONAL top-level array naming the material-change patches this interpretation proposes to the Applier, per pipeline-doctrine Appendix A. Under null_cycle the prompt contract requires an empty array (never omission). Each patch carries the 5 Appendix A required fields: rule_applied, evidence_ids, confidence, material_change_justification, target_kb_path. The Applier is the authoritative rejector of malformed patches; this schema block performs structural gatekeeping only.

rule_applied.description: The methodology rule invoked to justify this patch. Free-form string until the enum is formalised (Sprint 4).

evidence_ids.description: Array of evidence IDs from the weekly-research bundle or KB that support the patch. Must be non-empty.

material_change_justification.description: One-sentence rationale for why this change is material (not cosmetic rewording of existing KB state).

target_kb_path.description: The KB path being updated (e.g. 'module_2.models[Anthropic/Claude Opus 4.6]' or 'module_7.vectors[ai_fimi_layer].rating').

patch_id.description: Optional stable patch ID. Applier may stamp one if absent.

operation.description: Optional Appendix A operation. Applier defaults to 'update' if absent.

prior_value.description: Optional prior-value snapshot for optimistic concurrency. Applier rejects if current KB state does not match.

new_value.description: Optional new value the patch applies.

ongoing_lab_postures.description: Safety and governance posture assessments for frontier AI labs.

gpai_compliance.description: GPAI compliance record — keyed by 'labs', containing per-lab compliance booleans.

open_findings.description: Salient AI-governance findings from the weekly research bundle not captured in other structured fields.
